name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit similar-asset lane for testing trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. The edge should come from reset-and-resume structure with broad participation thresholds rather than microscopic touch entries.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset setups are too rare, if pullback and continuation filters conflict, or if the resumed leg fails to survive costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional move resets more deeply into value, then resumes once the reset is accepted instead of rolling over further.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When directional pressure pauses, deeper pullbacks can reload the move more effectively than shallow trend-following entries, especially if acceptance back above structure is clear.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is a clean similar-asset lane because reset-and-resume behavior is common enough on this canvas to preserve structure while still allowing meaningful pullback depth.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains purely rotational without genuine reset acceptance, continuation entries will be early and get faded.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but resumed legs are too short, cut runner ambition before tightening the reset depth requirement.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reset and continuation roles are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reset thresholds instead of exact EMA-touch logic
